understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to place all selectable puzzle pieces onto the goal tiles (marked '+') simultaneously. Winning required all goals to be occupied at once before running out of steps.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The player could move a cursor around a grid using MOVE actions. CLICK on a selectable piece's coordinate would select it. Once selected, a subsequent CLICK on a goal tile would move the selected piece to that goal, provided the move was valid (e.g., not blocked by obstacles). The game had a limited number of steps per level; if steps expired without all goals being filled, the run ended in failure. In this experience, the repeated CLICK on [9,21] moved the selected piece from [18,21] each time, suggesting either the move was not valid/blocked or the piece did not stay at the goal, and the final out-of-bounds CLICK had no effect, causing step depletion.
